subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1** game board (5×5 grid)
- **5** red pawns (4 students + 1 master)
- **5** blue pawns (4 students + 1 master)
- **16** move cards (each showing a unique movement pattern)
- **1** game mat

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place board** between players.
2. **Each player** places their Master on the center of their back row and 4 Students on the remaining back row spaces.
3. **Shuffle move cards** — Deal 2 to each player and place 1 face up to the side (the waiting card).
4. **Starting player** — The color shown on the waiting card goes first.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **2 move cards** in front of you (visible to opponent).
- **5 pieces** on your back row.
- **Temple square** — Your master starts on the center space (your temple arch).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Onitama is a chess-like 2-player game on a 5×5 grid. Move pieces according to movement cards that rotate between players. Capture the opponent's master or move your master to their temple to win.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:
1. **Choose** one of your 2 move cards.
2. **Move** one of your pieces according to the card's pattern (relative to the piece's position).
3. **Capture** — If you land on an opponent's piece, remove it.
4. **Exchange card** — Place the used card to the side as the new waiting card. Take the previous waiting card into your hand.

You always have exactly 2 move cards.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Movement
- Each card shows a grid with possible moves relative to the piece.
- The pattern is oriented from the perspective of the player using it.
- You may move any of your 5 pieces (master or student).
- You must make a move if possible (you cannot pass).

#### Capturing
- Move onto a space occupied by an opponent's piece to capture it.
- Cannot land on your own pieces.

#### Card Rotation
- After using a card, it goes to the waiting position.
- You take the old waiting card.
- This means your opponent will eventually get the card you just used.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Perfect information** — All move cards are face up and visible.
- **Only 5 cards in play** — 2 per player + 1 waiting. The remaining 11 are unused.
- **No passing** — You must move if any legal move exists.
- **Board wrapping** — Movement patterns that go off the board are simply invalid.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win Conditions
- **Way of the Stone** — Capture your opponent's Master.
- **Way of the Stream** — Move your Master onto the opponent's Temple Arch (center of their back row).

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Read ahead** — Plan not just your move, but what cards your opponent will have next.
- **Protect your master** — Don't expose your master unnecessarily.
- **Two win conditions** — Always be aware of both capture and temple objectives.
- **Card cycling** — Remember that the card you use now goes to your opponent eventually.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Tempo control** — Sometimes it's better to move a student to give yourself a better card next turn.
- **Fork threats** — Position pieces to threaten both master capture and temple entry.
- **Card denial** — Use a powerful card even for a minor move to keep it cycling away from your opponent.
- **Center control** — Control the center of the 5×5 board for maximum flexibility.
